LAW AND MOTION, 302, RULING: THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING WHICH WAS NOT CONTESTED. DEFENDANT 2163 UNION CORPORATION, A CORPORATION, DBA NIDA'S MOTION TO SET ASIDE DEFAULT/DEFAULT JUDGMENT/LEAVE TO DEFEND IS DENIED. DEFENDANT FAILS TO MEET ITS BURDEN OF SHOWING DEMONSTRATE MISTAKE, EXCUSABLE NEGLECT, INADVERTENCE, OR SURPRISE WITHIN THE MEANING OF CCP 473. IT IS UNDISPUTED THAT DEFENDANT WAS PROPERLY SERVED WITH THE SUMMONS ON MAY 21. 2012. THERE IS NO EXPLANATION AS TO WHY DEFENDANT'S EMPLOYEE DID NOT PROVIDE THE SUMMONS TO ITS PRESIDENT MR. GIOBBIO, OR WHY DEFENDANT DID NOT OTHERWISE TAKE ANY ACTION UPON RECEIVING THE SUMMONS. DEFENDANT WAS SERVED WITH A REQUEST FOR ENTRY OF DEFAULT ON JULY 26, 2012. MR. GIOBBIO ADMITS HE PERSONALLY RECEIVED THE SUMMONS AND COMPLAINT IN AUGUST 2012 (APPARENTLY FROM HIS OFFICE), BUT FAILED TO ACT ON IT UNTIL MID- SEPTEMBER 2012 WHEN HE RETURNED TO SAN FRANCISCO. THERE IS NO EXPLANATION AS TO WHY DEFENDANT DID NOT RECEIVE A COPY OF THE REQUEST FOR ENTRY OF DEFAULT IN AUGUST AT THE SAME TIME HE RECEIVED THE SUMMONS AND COMPLAINT, SINCE IT HAD BEEN SERVED. HE DID NOT TAKE ANY STEPS TO DISCUSS THE SUMMONS AND COMPLAINT AND REQUEST FOR ENTRY OF DEFAULT WITH HIS ATTORNEY UNTIL SEPTEMBER 17, 2012. ON THESE FACTS, THE REQUISITE SHOWING FOR RELIEF FROM DEFAULT HAS NOT BEEN MADE. (THE COURT WILL REVIEW AND, IF APPROVED, SIGN THE PROPOSED ORDER EMAILED TO THE COURT.) JUDGE: MARLA J. MILLER; CLERK: GINA GONZALES; COURT REPORTER: ANTHONY C. VAUGHN, CSR # 6185

calendar.matters:
CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar May-28-2013 Off Calendar

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Jan-31-2013 Continued To Jun-12-13 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

MOTION TO SET ASIDE DEFAULT/DEFAULT JUDGMENT/LEAVE TO DEFEND
Off Calendar Jan-25-2013 Denied
MINI MINUTES:
No appearance on this matter.

RULING: The Court adopted its tentative ruling which was not contested. DEFENDANT 2163 UNION CORPORATION, A CORPORATION, DBA NIDA's MOTION TO SET ASIDE DEFAULT/DEFAULT JUDGMENT/LEAVE TO DEFEND is denied. Defendant fails to meet its burden of showing demonstrate mistake, excusable neglect, inadvertence, or surprise within the meaning of CCP 473. It is undisputed that Defendant was properly served with the summons on May 21. 2012. There is no explanation as to why Defendant's employee did not provide the summons to its president Mr. Giobbio, or why Defendant did not otherwise take any action upon receiving the summons. Defendant was served with a request for entry of default on July 26, 2012. Mr. Giobbio admits he personally received the summons and complaint in August 2012 (apparently from his office), but failed to act on it until mid- September 2012 when he returned to San Francisco. There is no explanation as to why Defendant did not receive a copy of the request for entry of default in August at the same time he received the summons and complaint, since it had been served. He did not take any steps to discuss the summons and complaint and request for entry of default with his attorney until September 17, 2012. On these facts, the requisite showing for relief from default has not been made. (The court will review and, if approved, sign the proposed order emailed to the court.) Judge: Marla J. Miller; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Court Reporter: Anthony C. Vaughn, CSR # 6185
